# SL-INDEX-004: robots-blocks-indexable-route

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

robots.txt blocks crawling for a route declared indexable.

## Metadata

- Category: Indexability (indexability)
- Default severity: blocker
- Confidence: certain
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- robots-txt
- crawler

## Required Evidence

- route-contract
- robots-rule
- matched-url

## When This Triggers

Match URL against robots rules for configured user agents and compare with route
contract.

## False Positives

False positives are expected to be rare because this rule relies on
deterministic local evidence. Suppress only with a reason when the route
contract intentionally differs from the observed signal.

## How To Fix

Allow crawling for indexable URLs or mark the route non-indexable.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/indexability/robots-blocks-indexable

Fixed direction:

- Allow crawling for indexable URLs or mark the route non-indexable.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

## Implementation Surfaces

- Crawler snapshots can provide crawl, robots.txt, sitemap, and site-graph
  evidence.
